title: Interfaces, Dynamic Types, Errors, and API Boundaries · Execution Trace: Interface Satisfaction
summary: A notification service must accept narrow capabilities, preserve error causes, and distinguish nil interfaces from typed nil values. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go interface satisfaction increment in a capability-oriented notification boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define small consumer-owned interfaces and use implicit satisfaction without premature abstraction.
Compose small types and constructors so invalid state, ownership, and transitions remain observable and testable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
